end

def solve(grid, x, y, k)
  new_grid = grid.map { |row| row.dup }
  submatrix = []
  (x..x + k - 1).each do |i|
    submatrix << new_grid[i][y...y + k]
  end
  reversed_submatrix = submatrix.reverse
  (x..x + k - 1).each do |i|
    row_index = i - x
    reversed_row = reversed_submatrix[row_index]
    (0...k).each do |j|
      new_grid[i][y + j] = reversed_row[j]
    end
  end
  new_grid
end
```

The function solve takes grid, x, y, k.

function solve(grid: number[][], x: number, y: number, k: number): number[][] {

    // Create a copy of the grid

    const newGrid = grid.map(row => [...row]);

    // Extract the submatrix rows

    const submatrixRows = [];

    for (let i = x; i < x + k; i++) {

        const row = newGrid[i].slice(y, y + k);

        submatrixRows.push(row);

    }

    // Reverse the submatrix rows

    submatrixRows.reverse();

    // Replace the rows in newGrid

    for (let i = x; i < x + k; i++) {

        const reversedRow = submatrixRows[i - x];

        for (let j = 0; j < k; j++) {

            newGrid[i][y + j] = reversedRow[j];

        }

    }

    return newGrid;

}
